steps.title: Read what the two halves say
steps.content: First half: 2023 measurements showed the tower was rotating in a concerning way. Second half (after the blank): city officials closed the area so experts could explore solutions to stabilize the structure. The closure is a direct response to the concerning measurements.

steps.title: Pick the transition that names cause and effect
steps.content: Concerning movement is the cause; closing the area to investigate is the effect. The transition must mark that this action follows from the prior finding.

steps.title: Eliminate by rule
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Similarly,': signals two parallel cases, but the passage gives one finding and one response, not two analogous situations. Misread of the relationship. ✗
  • Choice B — 'As a result,': marks the closure as the consequence of the concerning rotation — exactly the cause-effect link in the text. ✓
  • Choice C — 'For example,': would introduce an instance of a general claim, but the first sentence is itself a specific finding, not a generalization needing illustration. Misread. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In comparison,': signals a comparison between two things, but only one tower and one response are discussed. Overreach. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
